title: Add a per-resource concurrent-booking limit
description: bookit currently admits at most one active reservation per resource for any time slot: any overlap with a pending or confirmed reservation is rejected. Product wants configurable concurrency: a resource should be able to accept up to a configurable number of simultaneous overlapping reservations - for example a four-lane pool that four parties can book independently for the same hour. Add a per-resource concurrent-booking limit, configurable when the resource is created, enforced at reservation creation, with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Resource creation accepts an optional per-resource concurrent-booking limit; the limit is a positive integer.
When no limit is supplied, behavior is unchanged from today: at most one active reservation per slot.

With a limit of N, up to N overlapping pending-or-confirmed reservations are admitted for the same slot and the N+1st overlapping request is rejected with HTTP 409, code slot_unavailable, using the structured error envelope.
Cancelled and completed reservations do not count toward the limit.
The limit is enforced per resource: overlapping reservations on different resources are independent.
Enforcement is correct under concurrent requests: a burst of simultaneous requests never admits more than N overlapping reservations.
Any schema change is a new, higher-numbered migration file; existing files under internal/store/migrations/ are untouched.
New tests cover the default, the exact limit boundary (N admitted, N+1 rejected), non-counting of inactive reservations, and the concurrent burst.
go test ./... and go test -race ./... pass.
constraints: Follow AGENTS.md conventions: business rules in internal/booking, thin handlers and SQL, structured error envelope, testify/assert, deterministic tests.
Backward compatibility: existing API requests without the new field must behave exactly as before.
Do not edit existing migration files.
non_goals: Per-reservation party sizes or partial-capacity bookings.
Updating or reflowing existing stored data.
An endpoint for changing the limit after resource creation.
Frontend or client changes (bookit is API-only).

visible_validation.description: Full suite passes, including the new feature tests.
Suite passes as CI runs it, including the concurrent-burst test.
Schema changes, if any, appear only as new migration files.

expected_artifacts: A diff against the starting revision implementing the feature
New tests covering the limit semantics and the concurrent burst
A short design note stating the decisions taken on the known ambiguities

human_review_rubric.criterion: Limit semantics correct at the boundary and under concurrency
Defaulting preserves existing behavior exactly (no hidden regressions)
Domain rule placed in internal/booking; layering and migration rules respected
Ambiguities resolved explicitly in the design note rather than silently
Cost and iteration count proportionate to a small feature

known_ambiguities: Resources already have a capacity field whose relationship to the new limit is unspecified: reusing it or adding a distinct field are both defensible - decide and justify in the design note.
How GET /resources/{id}/availability should present slots that are partially booked but still below the limit is unspecified; document the chosen semantics.
Whether the limit appears in the resource response DTO is open; if it does, it must not break existing clients that reject unknown fields.
expected_failure_modes: Enforcing the limit only sequentially so a concurrent burst exceeds N.
Changing default behavior for resources created without a limit.
Editing an existing migration instead of adding a new one.
parallelization_opportunities: Domain-rule implementation and API/store plumbing can be developed by separate workers once the design note fixes the field semantics.
